name: pymatgen (Materials Science Python Library)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Materials Project / Lawrence Berkeley National Lab

auth.notes: Materials Project API key required for online database access; local use is free

performance.notes: Local computation fast; Materials Project API calls are network-bound

agent_readiness.notes: Comprehensive materials science analysis toolkit. from pymatgen.core import Structure, Lattice. struct = Structure.from_file('POSCAR'). struct.volume. struct.get_neighbors(site, r=3.0). Composition('Fe2O3').weight. Phase diagram: PhaseDiagram(entries). Materials Project API: from mp_api.client import MPRester. mpr = MPRester(api_key). docs = mpr.materials.summary.search(elements=['Fe', 'O']). Electronic structure: BandStructure, DosPlotter. Symmetry: SpacegroupAnalyzer. Surface: SlabGenerator. Electrochemistry: SubstrateAnalyzer. Used in high-throughput DFT workflows.

gotchas: Steep learning curve — requires materials science background
Materials Project API has rate limits without subscription
VASP/Quantum ESPRESSO output parsing requires those codes installed
Version compatibility with emmet-core and mp-api can be tricky
best_when: High-throughput DFT calculations and materials database queries
Phase diagram computation for materials stability
ML feature generation for materials property prediction
avoid_when: Molecular chemistry (use RDKit or ASE for molecules)
General scientific Python without materials focus
summary: pymatgen is the Python materials science library for the Materials Project. Structure.from_file('POSCAR') reads crystal structures. SpacegroupAnalyzer for symmetry. Phase diagram computation. MPRester(api_key) queries 154k+ materials in the Materials Project database. Electronic structure, surface, electrochemistry analysis. High-throughput DFT workflow automation. MIT license. Requires materials science background. Essential for computational materials science in Python.
